mission.title: pushgateway: Consider not assigning an instance label if none is requested
mission.description: Consider not assigning an instance label if none is requested. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current pushgateway deployment
steps.description:
Verify your pushgateway version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
helm list -n pushgateway 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working pushgateway installation.

steps.title: Review pushgateway configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant pushgateway configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
kubectl get configmap -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=pushgateway
```
Currently, we add the ip number:
https://github.com/prometheus/pushgateway/blob/master/handler/push.go#L55

Cf. https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/490

steps.title: Apply the fix for Consider not assigning an instance label if none is…
steps.description:
Sending to a pushgateway on localhost, without specifying an instance field, caused pushgateway to fill in `instance="["`. This is because the IP address is `[::1]`, whereas the code assumes an IPv4 address. Using the standard `net.SplitHostPort` is the right thing, and it produces the right results (namely, `instance="::1"`).

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
kubectl get events -n pushgateway --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Consider not assigning an instance label if none is…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Sending to a pushgateway on localhost, without specifying an instance field, caused pushgateway to fill in `instance="["`. This is because the IP address is `[::1]`, whereas the code assumes an IPv4 address. Using the standard `net.SplitHostPort` is the right thing, and it produces the right results (namely, `instance="::1"`).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with pushgateway installed or the issue environment reproducible.
